articles.title: A Tiny Dinosaur That Changes Science
articles.summary: Scientists found a very old dinosaur fossil in Argentina. It is called Alnashetri and is about 90 million years old. This dinosaur was very small, weighing less than 2 pounds. It belongs to a group called alvarezsaurs, which had short arms and big thumb claws. Before this discovery, scientists did not know much about these dinosaurs because fossils from South America were not complete. The new fossil is almost whole, so it helps scientists understand how these dinosaurs evolved and became small. It shows that alvarezsaurs were already tiny before they developed special features for eating ants. The fossil was found in a place called La Buitrera, which has many other important fossils like early snakes. This discovery helps solve a mystery about dinosaur evolution.

articles.title: A New Mineral Found on Mars
articles.summary: Scientists have found a new mineral on Mars that might be brand-new. It is called iron sulfate and was discovered near a big canyon called Valles Marineris. Mars is very dry, so minerals like this can stay for billions of years and tell us about the planet's past. Researchers used data from spacecraft to study the mineral. They think it formed when water evaporated and left behind layers. Heat from volcanoes or lava might have changed the mineral later. This discovery helps us learn more about Mars' history and if it had water long ago.

articles.title: Empty Space That Pushes the Universe Apart
articles.summary: Cosmic voids are huge empty areas in space that look like nothing is there, but they are not truly empty. They are filled with something called spacetime vacuum, which has energy. This energy is called dark energy, and it makes the universe expand faster. In most places, like Earth or galaxies, matter is so strong that dark energy doesn't do much. But in voids, where there is very little matter, dark energy becomes powerful and pushes space apart. This means voids are where the universe grows bigger the most. Scientists study voids to understand dark energy better and learn how the universe changes over time.

articles.title: A University Helps the UK Space Industry
articles.summary: The University of Southampton has started a new space institute in Hampshire to help the UK's space sector, which is worth £17 billion each year. This institute brings together experts to work on new space technology and make space more sustainable. It will train students, create jobs, and support businesses in the area. The university has a long history of teaching about aircraft and satellites since 1959. Recently, scientists got over a million pounds to build a plasma torch for testing spacecraft heat and a water-based engine for satellites. The goal is to grow the economy and make the UK a leader in space education and science.

articles.title: Colorblindness and Bladder Cancer: A Hidden Danger
articles.summary: Scientists from Stanford found that colorblindness can make it harder to spot blood in urine, which is a warning sign for bladder cancer. People with colorblindness often cannot see red colors well, so they might not notice blood. This can lead to delays in seeing a doctor. The study looked at health records and discovered that bladder cancer patients with colorblindness had a 52% higher chance of dying over 20 years compared to those with normal vision. The researchers want to raise awareness so that colorblind people and doctors know about this risk. Colorblindness affects about 1 in 12 men and 1 in 200 women, and bladder cancer is more common in men. Early detection is key to better treatment and survival.

articles.title: Why Oil Prices Affect Everyone
articles.summary: Oil is a very important resource that we use for many things like cars and heating. A war in the Middle East is making it harder to get oil from that area, which is causing oil prices to go up. This means people pay more for gasoline and other goods. About 20% of the world's oil comes through a place called the Strait of Hormuz, but the war is blocking ships there. Other countries cannot make enough extra oil to help. This shortage is making some places in Asia and Europe worry about not having enough energy. Leaders are trying to fix the problem, but it is very big. Oil prices matter because they affect how much things cost for everyone around the world.
